167126.explanation: Depending on the topic and impact, it is good to involve residents in decisions. This can be through citizen assemblies, but also via direct input from residents, entrepreneurs, and expert organizations. The form should fit the topic, with clear agreements. The municipal council ultimately decides.

167127.explanation: To keep housing costs affordable, we do not want an increase in property tax (ozb).

167128.explanation: Public grass should primarily be mowed intensively in functional places (play areas, dog off-leash fields, along bike paths, and at intersections). To promote biodiversity (insects, plants), a tailored mowing policy is needed in other locations.

167130.explanation: BurgerBelangen wants safety to be visible again in daily life. This requires more neighborhood police officers and municipal enforcement officers (boa’s) on the streets, also in the evenings and on weekends.

167131.explanation: A clean living environment is important for the livability of our city. Unfortunately, a lot of waste is still dumped illegally. Therefore, we choose a firm and effective approach to waste dumping, with higher fines and camera surveillance where necessary.

167132.explanation: We want lighting on dark and unsafe places, such as parks, tunnels, and alleys. Street intimidation is also tackled firmly: everyone must feel free and safe on the street. Camera surveillance should be expanded where it helps to detect nuisance and crime faster.

167133.explanation: Fatbikes and fast bicycles do not belong in busy shopping areas. We follow the example of cities that have already taken this step for a safer center. We want to make shared spaces (shared use by bicycles and pedestrians) safer and prioritize pedestrians in the shopping area.

167134.explanation: First-time homebuyers should receive extra help through starter loans and be given priority in affordable projects, especially if they come from the municipality and have a social bond.

167135.explanation: It is about quality, affordability, and a healthy mix of homes in all neighborhoods. At least 30% must be affordable for starters, young people, and families; ranging from studios to smaller single-family homes and mid-range rentals. The current share of social rental housing of at least 30% fits within this ambition.

167136.explanation: To address the housing shortage, converting vacant buildings into residential units is an important measure. The municipality can stimulate the conversion of vacant properties through targeted subsidies (for example, a non-profitable top subsidy).

167137.explanation: The growth of Hengelo must happen carefully, so we want building according to need. Expansion on the edges of the city must not come at the expense of nature areas, but can take place on freed agricultural land or vacant terrain.

167138.explanation: Poverty often affects multiple areas of life (health, work, family). Higher thresholds ensure that working people/families who often fall just above the current limit but are still financially strained are eligible for help (e.g., exemption from fees).

167139.explanation: Socially meaningful work during a welfare benefit period can be a step toward paid employment. This social work can also be voluntary work with a volunteer allowance that is not deducted from the welfare benefits.

167143.explanation: Cash payment must remain an option, also at municipal counters. A national bill will require businesses and municipalities to accept cash up to a certain amount, probably starting in 2027.

167144.explanation: BurgerBelangen stands for acceptance and safety for everyone. Attention to LGBTQIA+ residents is important. This can be through a Pride week, but it does not have to be every year. Also through other actions and activities, the municipality can contribute to inclusion and connection.

167146.explanation: Good accessibility belongs to an attractive city center. Therefore, we want to further expand Shop & Go parking (30 minutes free parking).

167147.explanation: This aligns with Socially Responsible Procurement (MVI), where not only the lowest price but also social aspects are considered. Hengelo (as a frontrunner in broad prosperity) often takes social criteria into account.

167148.explanation: This is established in the policy for Socially Responsible Procurement (MVI), where besides price and quality, social aspects and environmental effects carry significant weight. The goal is for the government to serve as a role model and to stimulate the market for sustainability.

167149.explanation: We want the municipality to work as efficiently as possible and limit the use of expensive external contractors. This will give us control over rising costs and reduce dependence on contractors.

167150.explanation: Through political pressure and cooperation with healthcare parties (such as MST and ZGT), we want to advocate that Hengelo plays an important role in the future of regional healthcare. Hengelo can profile itself with the Health Park as a logical location with good accessibility.

167151.explanation: Hengelo already accommodates more asylum seekers than legally required at the current shelter location on Hazenweg. The municipality of Hengelo serves as an example for other municipalities with this sustainable shelter.

167152.explanation: If a legal obligation requires this, Hengelo must comply. With the current housing possibilities on Hazenweg, we already make an important contribution.

167153.explanation: We want to integrate status holders housed in Hengelo as quickly as possible so they can immediately contribute to our society (for example, by working or volunteering from day one). Faster integration can reduce total integration costs.

167154.explanation: A district heating network can only be implemented if it is feasible, affordable, and future-proof. Without broad support, the municipality should not push it through. Residents must retain freedom of choice and must not be obligated. The current gas network must remain available for alternatives such as green gas and hydrogen.

167156.explanation: We want to gradually switch to residual waste separation (plastic and residual waste in one bag/container). First in high-rise buildings and eventually also in low-rise, once Twence has a modern residual waste sorting facility that achieves better results (environmentally and cost-wise) than the current source separation.

167157.explanation: A fixed waste fee is clear and fair. The current price incentive does not work well and leads to contamination of waste streams and sometimes dumping. In the end, residents pay these extra costs together. A fixed amount can prevent this.

167159.explanation: We do not want this nuisance-causing industry close to residential neighborhoods. Failure to meet environmental requirements and the resulting ever-increasing fines (now already 1 million euros) show that there is no right to exist at the current location. We will reserve the fines we collect for relocation or potential buy-out.

167160.explanation: We want a greener and cooler Hengelo. Trees may only be removed if they are replaced, preferably in the same neighborhood. In neighborhoods with a lot of heat stress, we will address the consequences of heat nuisance and heavy rainfall targetedly: more greenery, fewer tiles, and smart places to catch rainwater.

167161.explanation: Hengelo does not have a general environmental zone (zero-emission zone) for the city center but already has a completely car-free downtown with strict access rules and camera enforcement.

167162.explanation: The province decides on the number of wind turbines, not the municipality. So far, there is one wind turbine planned. If a wind turbine is installed, we always want to apply the strictest possible standards to minimize inconvenience for local residents, nature, and the landscape.

167163.explanation: Art and culture give Hengelo color, identity, and connection. They make the city attractive to live, work, and grow up in. BurgerBelangen wants this cultural foundation to remain strong and be able to grow.

167164.explanation: Many school buildings are outdated, resulting in poor indoor climate and high energy costs. Therefore, we want to invest extra in these locations.

167165.explanation: Every child should have the opportunity to take music lessons, regardless of their parents' income. Music and culture contribute to development and self-confidence. The municipality has a role in making these opportunities possible for children from low-income families.

167167.explanation: We have a nice, centrally located official skatepark that provides a safe place for skaters. Its central location behind the station prevents nuisance in residential neighborhoods. However, we want to involve young people, schools, and local residents more in designing new play and exercise areas.

167168.explanation: We want to give groups of residents the opportunity to develop new forms of housing together. This can also include elderly people who want to build homes together and thus stimulate housing flow.

167169.explanation: Homes without parking spaces often cause extra parking pressure in neighborhoods. Parking standards should fit the neighborhood and the type of housing. In the inner city or Heart of South, for example, this can be managed with parking in (nearby) parking garages via subscriptions. This way, livability is preserved.

167170.explanation: Of the total housing construction, 30% must be social rental housing, and two-thirds of the new homes should be for low- and middle-income earners. We want to build according to demand, so there must also be sufficient choice in the middle segment.

167171.explanation: Hengelo offers (financial) support to Welbions with schemes (such as subsidies) for new construction of social and affordable rental homes to mitigate rising construction costs.

167172.explanation: Many residents with low incomes do not know which schemes are available to them. The information is unclear or hard to find. Therefore, we want to simplify application procedures and have more social-legal advisors available who can help.

167173.explanation: Young people grow up in a time full of extra risks: debts, online temptations, vaping, drugs, peer pressure, and criminal recruitment. We want structural attention for resilience, including learning how to manage money with early detection of debts.

167174.explanation: Traffic safety is always urgent. Therefore, among other things, we want separated bike lanes along all 50 km/h roads and standard 30 km/h zones in residential areas.

167175.explanation: We want lower rates during off-peak hours and free public transport to the city center on Saturdays. There is already a free bus subscription for low-income seniors aged 65+, and there is an investigation into expanding this to all low-income residents with funding via the region/province.

167176.explanation: Improving bike paths, such as installing better paving or making crossings safer, is essential to reduce accidents and can be prioritized given the ambition to make Hengelo as bicycle-friendly as possible.

167177.explanation: We want to keep public transport accessible and affordable, with lower fares during off-peak hours and free public transport to the city center on Saturdays.

167178.explanation: Free parking encourages visits to the city center. BurgerBelangen is open to a trial with the first hour free, combined with an expansion of Shop&Go spots and in good coordination with Q-Park to avoid unfair competition and legal issues.

167179.explanation: The extension of Laan van Driene is part of the municipal mobility plan to improve traffic flow and make the city more accessible. It will also greatly reduce cut-through traffic in the surrounding neighborhoods.

167180.explanation: Meeting places play an increasingly important role. Not only large district centers are important, but also smaller meeting places and low-threshold living rooms in every neighborhood and district.

167181.explanation: Homeless people must receive good shelter and guidance. Automatic priority for social rental housing is not fair to other housing seekers. It is better to prevent homelessness by offering earlier tailored support during problems so that people do not become homeless.

167182.explanation: Hengelo has its own complaints procedure, and residents can contact the National (provincial) Ombudsman if they are dissatisfied with the handling. For such complaint handling, a local ombudsman could be a valuable addition.

167126.explanation: We consider citizen participation important. Therefore, this is a key priority for us. A citizens' assembly increases the support for decisions.

167127.explanation: The increase in property tax (ozb) affects those who struggle the most to a lesser extent. These revenues may be necessary to maintain social services.

167128.explanation: Ecological mowing is better for biodiversity and the livability of our municipality.

167130.explanation: BESPOKE OFFICERS can make an important contribution to maintaining social safety.

167131.explanation: We believe that fines do not contribute to solving the litter problem.

167132.explanation: A good measure to promote social safety.

167133.explanation: Fatbikes cause too much nuisance, so where legally possible, they should be banned from the city center.

167134.explanation: There must be a fair distribution among all groups eligible for social rental housing. Our residents do not need to receive priority but are, of course, part of this group.

167135.explanation: There is an enormous shortage of affordable housing; this increases the number of affordable homes.

167136.explanation: There is a huge housing shortage. We must do everything possible to resolve this shortage as quickly and efficiently as possible. Repurposing existing real estate is a good idea.

167137.explanation: We must do everything to add homes within the built-up area. The city contains sufficient plots with building possibilities, where we can also go up in height well without harming livability. Only if really necessary will we broaden our outlook by building outside the built-up area.

167138.explanation: Even people who are just above the income limit find it difficult to make ends meet.

167139.explanation: This is not the intention of the social assistance benefit. People do not choose to be on social assistance.

167143.explanation: On the one hand, we are for safety and therefore digital payments are better. On the other hand, there is still a group of residents who prefer to pay cash.

167144.explanation: Pride contributes to an inclusive society where everyone can be themselves and is accepted.

167146.explanation: We encourage visiting the city by bike and public transport.

167147.explanation: It contributes to a better society.

167148.explanation: Where it is sustainable, we must do it sustainably. We can steer this with our purchasing policy. As a municipality, you must also set a good example.

167149.explanation: In principle, permanent staff should always be deployed. If it really cannot be helped, external parties should be hired.

167150.explanation: Hengelo has a central place in Twente and is therefore a logically accessible location.

167151.explanation: We are already doing that and it is going very well.

167152.explanation: All target groups must be served, but these people have no home. And people without a home must be given priority.

167153.explanation: If you want refugees to be able to contribute to society, you must do everything possible to support them.

167154.explanation: We need to take steps in the energy transition. The most important thing is that it remains affordable for residents and that they can choose whether to connect.

167156.explanation: Separating waste at the source works well in low-rise buildings. It contributes to a sustainable and affordable waste system.

167157.explanation: This increases it for everyone, including single-person households or people who handle their waste very carefully.

167159.explanation: We are not against an asphalt plant as such, because asphalt has to be produced somewhere. However, the producer must comply with environmental and safety standards. If enforcement does not have the desired effect, it will have to close anyway.

167160.explanation: More nature and green spaces in the city increase livability and biodiversity.

167161.explanation: It increases livability and is better for the environment.

167162.explanation: A condition for wind turbines must always be that there are no health risks, no noise nuisance, and no inconvenience for residents. But wind turbines can contribute well to the energy transition and take up less space than solar fields.

167163.explanation: Art and culture are incredibly important for a pleasant municipality to live in.

167164.explanation: This helps with the energy transition and saves money in the long term. In addition, it also makes the indoor climate more pleasant for students and teachers.

167165.explanation: Music contributes greatly to the development of children and everyone should have this opportunity.

167167.explanation: This invites young people to go outside, be active, and meet each other in a positive way.

167168.explanation: We wholeheartedly support these kinds of initiatives; they can also promote the flow in the housing market.

167169.explanation: Where it is not necessary, we should use public space for greenery.

167170.explanation: This does not serve the whole society and promotes less flow. We must focus on a broad and diverse supply with sufficient affordable rental and owner-occupied housing.

167171.explanation: This is a group that often falls between the cracks. There must also be enough homes for them.

167172.explanation: The municipality's task is to support residents where needed. This can prevent problems from getting worse.

167173.explanation: It is undesirable for people to be in debt at a young age. Therefore, we must also do everything to support them.

167174.explanation: This is good for traffic safety.

167175.explanation: This increases the mobility of people who depend on public transport; it can also help prevent loneliness.

167176.explanation: It is important that maintenance is done well everywhere. It must therefore be addressed equally, also for safety reasons.

167177.explanation: This encourages the use of public transport.

167179.explanation: We see no added value here; we can better allocate resources elsewhere.

167180.explanation: It is important that a neighborhood itself feels the need for a community center. Therefore, it goes too far to say in advance that every neighborhood must have a community center.

167181.explanation: The best solution for homelessness is to give people a roof over their heads.

167182.explanation: This increases trust in the government and it is important to be able to express complaints about or to the municipality in an accessible way.

167126.explanation: The municipal council is directly elected by our residents. Additionally, there are already several advisory bodies the municipality works with. Advice should come from experts, not by lottery.

167127.explanation: We accept increases in local taxes but not in the property tax (OZB). Therefore, we will ensure these taxes remain as low as possible for both residents and entrepreneurs.

167128.explanation: We think it is important that public spaces are clean and accessible.

167130.explanation: Enforcement officers tackle minor offenses and recover damages from offenders, with fine revenues returning to the municipality as much as possible.

167131.explanation: Street litterers must be tackled more strictly.

167132.explanation: Everyone should be able to walk the streets safely while just being themselves. We achieve this with targeted camera surveillance that respects your privacy and with good lighting when you are out. Safety is not just about numbers, but also about the feelings of our residents.

167133.explanation: We want to tackle the nuisance caused by fatbikes. A ban could help with that.

167134.explanation: Although we think it is important that every resident of Hengelo gets suitable housing, this is legally not possible.

167135.explanation: We must continuously monitor how many homes are needed and in which segments. We build not according to percentages, but according to what is necessary.

167136.explanation: Because the housing shortage is high, we must explore all possibilities to build more homes.

167137.explanation: Because the housing shortage is high, we must explore all possibilities to build more homes. We will continue to keep an eye on our Twente green spaces.

167138.explanation: Raising the poverty line does not solve the problem. People who work must see that reflected in their wallets. That means lowering taxes.

167139.explanation: People who temporarily cannot work due to circumstances may count on municipal support. We do expect that people receiving benefits give something back to society through a mandatory counter-performance.

167143.explanation: Cash is legal tender. The government must adhere to its own laws.

167144.explanation: We have respect for LGBTQIA+ residents. We support an inclusive society where everyone feels at home and safe.

167146.explanation: An attractive city center requires an attractive parking policy.

167147.explanation: The municipality of Hengelo should purchase as much as possible from Hengelo-based businesses.

167148.explanation: The municipality of Hengelo should purchase as much as possible from Hengelo-based businesses.

167149.explanation: External personnel is generally very expensive. We must invest in internal knowledge.

167150.explanation: Good healthcare must be nearby. However, it remains the hospital's own choice.

167151.explanation: Hengelo takes its responsibility, but we do not need to take over the responsibilities of other municipalities.

167152.explanation: Everyone must have an equal chance of obtaining housing, regardless of their background.

167153.explanation: Integration starts with the newcomer themselves. The municipality facilitates this.

167154.explanation: We are critical of mandatory systems, such as heat networks. Our criteria remain: feasible, affordable, and freedom of choice. Support from the community is very important in this.

167156.explanation: In recent years, waste disposal for Hengelo residents has become increasingly difficult. The system is complex due to separation of residual waste and plastic & cans (packaging/PMD), while better technical solutions already exist, such as post-separation.

167157.explanation: We believe in the polluter pays principle. This means that if you produce more waste, you pay more.

167159.explanation: As long as the asphalt plant does not meet environmental requirements, we believe the plant should be closed. The costs must be borne by the asphalt plant, not the residents.

167160.explanation: Green streets not only create a pleasant living environment, but also contribute to cooling in summer, better water retention during heavy rain, and healthier air quality.

167162.explanation: It's not about numbers, but about achieving our sustainability goals. Community support from our residents is very important in this.

167163.explanation: Subsidies for cultural and artistic facilities support a wide range of offerings for as many residents as possible. The Hengelo club community is the most logical partner in this society.

167164.explanation: We find it important to invest in safe, cleaner, and multifunctional school buildings.

167165.explanation: We already have facilities for this here in Hengelo.

167167.explanation: We are proud of the facilities that already exist, such as at the back of the station.

167168.explanation: We support initiatives that lead to more housing for every target group.

167169.explanation: Every home deserves at least its own parking space.

167170.explanation: We must build according to needs, not percentages. We help starters to buy a home. We support starters by providing municipal starter loans and building affordable houses that meet the needs of starters and families.

167171.explanation: We already collaborate well with Welbions. Housing construction is Welbions' task, and the municipality can support where Welbions runs into issues.

167172.explanation: Work is the best social security. Work offers people social contacts, structure, security, and money. People who temporarily cannot work due to circumstances may count on municipal support.

167173.explanation: We see more and more young people with debts. If we can detect this early, we can prevent worse problems in the future.

167174.explanation: Where 50 km/h is sufficiently safe, we should not reduce it to 30 km/h.

167176.explanation: We prioritize safety for pedestrian and bicycle paths as well as roads for cars.

167177.explanation: Nothing is free. Ultimately, residents pay for it through taxes. We prefer to invest money in making our city center more attractive.

167178.explanation: An attractive city center requires an attractive parking policy.

167179.explanation: To improve traffic flow, we must invest in Laan van Driene. Cars stopped in traffic jams are not good for the environment.

167180.explanation: Every neighborhood has its own needs. We must respond to the needs of residents in each neighborhood.

167181.explanation: Everyone must have an equal chance of obtaining housing, regardless of their background.

167182.explanation: We stand for a service-oriented government. This means that if there are complaints about the government, residents must be able to express them.

167126.explanation: The SP finds it very important that all residents are involved as much as possible in matters concerning their neighborhood, district, or municipality. The municipality must ensure this. However, we are not in favor of lotteries, because not everyone who wants to can participate then.

167127.explanation: The SP does not want to increase the property tax (OZB) to free up extra money for new municipal policies. We only want to increase the OZB in line with the applicable price index.

167128.explanation: Allowing grass to grow longer gives herbs and flowers more chance to grow and bloom. This attracts insects. That is why the SP supports letting grass grow longer before mowing in places where it is safe to do so.

167130.explanation: The number of enforcement officers is currently sufficient. In places where safety is a concern, cooperation with, for example, police and housing corporations is needed. Additional officers alone do not immediately solve problems. We prefer to invest in prevention, such as more youth work.

167131.explanation: People who deliberately dump large amounts of waste should be dealt with strictly, for example with higher fines. However, a distinction must be made between antisocial dumpers and people who, for example, place a bag next to a broken container.

167132.explanation: Tunnels used only by cyclists and pedestrians, such as those under the A1 and the Grobbentunnel, can be equipped with cameras to deter potential offenders and thus increase safety and the feeling of safety. We will, however, be cautious in doing this.

167133.explanation: Fatbikes regularly cause unsafe situations and accidents, also because they are often illegally modified. If there is a legally permitted way to exclude these from the city center, we are open to it. However, so far no solution has been found that holds up legally.

167134.explanation: The SP believes that people should preferably live in the area where they also have their social and economic connections. In that sense, home seekers from the local municipality have priority.

167135.explanation: In recent years, the number of social rental homes has declined. Homes have been demolished or sold. Yet there is still a great demand, with waiting times lasting years. Therefore, agreements must be made to increase the share of social rental homes again.

167136.explanation: Some office buildings have been empty for many years while there is also a great shortage of homes. This is unacceptable. Therefore, it is good if vacant offices are converted into homes. If the municipality's contribution can make this possible, we are happy to cooperate.

167137.explanation: The SP is not in favor of expanding Hengelo beyond the current city boundaries. There are still enough empty spots within the city. We are also not in favor of the so-called 'scale jump' to 100,000 inhabitants. Hengelo's size is fine as it is now.

167138.explanation: At the moment, there is a 'poverty trap' for people who just fall above the thresholds for municipal support. This is undesirable because they then struggle to get by. Therefore, the municipality must step in as long as the national government does not fundamentally change income and benefits policies.

167139.explanation: People receiving benefits should be guided towards paid work if possible. We oppose working while retaining benefits. Work deserves a wage. If work is not possible, they can, for example, do volunteer work or follow training.

167143.explanation: Not everyone can or wants to pay by debit card. Cash is also a legal means of payment. Therefore, it is obvious that the municipality should always continue to accept it.

167144.explanation: Attention is still needed for LGBTQIA+ individuals because they are still regularly stigmatized or discriminated against. The municipality can draw attention to this at specific moments.

167146.explanation: We currently see no use or necessity for more of these places.

167147.explanation: A company should not only ensure economic returns but also positively influence the society in which it operates. These are social enterprises, and the municipality should appreciate this by purchasing from such companies.

167148.explanation: The municipality is a large organization and thus makes many purchases. The municipality has various ambitions regarding sustainability, so it makes perfect sense for it to set a good example in purchasing goods or services.

167149.explanation: In recent years, the hiring of external personnel has only increased. We consider this an undesirable development. It costs more money and the knowledge largely disappears when the external worker leaves.

167150.explanation: Hengelo is centrally and easily accessible in Twente. The hospitals in Almelo and Enschede are harder to reach. If at some point there is consideration for a single hospital location in Twente, it makes sense to consider Hengelo.

167151.explanation: People who have fled must be housed decently and safely. We support a fair distribution across municipalities. Currently, Hengelo already does more than necessary. Nevertheless, if Hengelo is called upon, we want to consider that seriously.

167152.explanation: This is the current policy. Status holders have the right to a home once recognized as refugees. Due to the shortage of affordable housing caused by right-wing government policies, groups of home seekers are pitted against each other. Only building more affordable housing offers a solution.

167153.explanation: It is in the interest of both status holders and society that people integrate and learn the language as quickly as possible. This allows them to become part of society sooner and find work faster. It is important that the municipality contributes to this.

167154.explanation: If neighborhoods must move away from natural gas, this means a major change for many people. If there is no support, for example, for connection to a heat network, this leads to great dissatisfaction. Therefore, it is important that residents support the choice made.

167156.explanation: The current way of waste separation leads to unacceptable messes. We want to switch to a system where waste is better sorted afterward, combined with a fair payment system.

167157.explanation: If a single fixed amount is paid, this means that people producing little waste pay for those producing a lot of waste. The SP finds this unfair. For many people, waste tax would then rise, which we do not want.

167159.explanation: The asphalt plant is located close to homes in South Hengelo. The company causes pollution, health risks, and nuisance to residents and exceeds limits for hazardous substance emissions. The buyout must not result in extra profit for the company.

167160.explanation: Nature is under heavy strain for various reasons. This is mostly caused by humans. Therefore, we consider it important to have projects that contribute to more greenery and biodiversity.

167161.explanation: Hengelo's city center cannot be compared to large, old cities like Amsterdam and Utrecht. Air quality in Hengelo is also much better than elsewhere. Additionally, except for loading and unloading, cars are no longer permitted in the city center.

167162.explanation: Research shows that there is little space in Hengelo for placing wind turbines. The SP believes the decision should be made more regionally rather than locally. We think there are better locations for wind turbines elsewhere.

167163.explanation: Art and culture form an important part of society. Many people enjoy them both professionally and as amateurs. With the theater, library, Metropool, and Oyfo, we also have excellent facilities. The SP does not cut funding for these.

167164.explanation: Many school buildings are somewhat old, and the indoor climate is not always healthy. By making them more sustainable, less energy is used, and children can receive education in a nicer and healthier environment.

167165.explanation: Every child deserves the chance to learn to play a musical instrument, including children whose parents have limited financial means. Every child should be able to participate in society. Therefore, it is good if those families receive support for sports and cultural activities.

167167.explanation: Hengelo has a thriving skate culture; therefore, the beautiful skate park near the station was opened a few years ago. If there is proven demand for more spots, we are positive about that but not necessarily in advance.

167168.explanation: However, the same conditions apply here as for other construction plans: also include social rental and affordable owner-occupied homes within the same plan.

167169.explanation: Parking spaces take up room, which is not always available, for example, in the city center and Hart van Zuid. If the living environment allows and no parking problems arise, we can agree to building homes/apartments without an associated parking space or mandatory garage storage.

167170.explanation: It is extremely difficult or even impossible for many people to get a home. Prices have risen absurdly in recent years. Therefore, the municipality must ensure that affordable homes are built so that young people and starters can also find a home.

167171.explanation: The municipality can only help Welbions to a limited extent because Welbions is an independent organization. If the municipality nonetheless provides financial support to enable certain projects, it should concern social rental homes with rents between 400 and 600 euros.

167172.explanation: Besides the municipality being generous in supporting residents with low incomes, applying for financial support must be simpler than it is now. The municipality must actively ensure that people know how to find and apply for the required support.

167173.explanation: People with debts often feel ashamed and therefore do not quickly seek help. As a result, help often only comes after the debt problem has worsened. It is therefore good if issues are identified early and help is offered.

167174.explanation: On certain roads where cyclists and cars share the same road, such as Josef Haydnlaan, Noordelijke Esweg, and Twekkelerweg, the speed limit is still 50 km/h. This regularly causes dangerous situations. Therefore, the maximum speed on such roads should be lowered.

167175.explanation: If people cannot afford to travel from A to B, this can cause feelings of isolation and loneliness. Therefore, the municipality must engage in talks with the bus operator to find a solution.

167176.explanation: Some bicycle paths are in poor condition, creating risks of accidents and/or injury. Cyclists are more vulnerable than motorists in this regard.

167177.explanation: It is still normal to pay to travel by bus. Therefore, there is no reason to exempt this on Saturdays. However, it is a good idea that people with low incomes can travel cheaply or for free by bus.

167178.explanation: The city center is easily accessible by bike or public transport; we want to encourage people to visit the city center this way. Cars are, of course, also welcome, but it must remain manageable.

167179.explanation: We oppose the costly construction of more asphalt without a good reason. Traffic flows reasonably well most of the day. Groot Driene would experience severe nuisance if the Laan van Driene were extended. We see absolutely no necessity or benefit in this.

167180.explanation: Community centers promote encounters between residents and organize various activities that people can participate in. They function best when they are close to residents so that less mobile people can easily reach them. Therefore, they must be opened in every neighborhood.

167181.explanation: Homelessness often arises from a buildup of problems and can happen to anyone. To provide people who have become homeless with a stable and safe foundation, housing is the most important. Besides housing, the municipality must also provide assistance in resolving the underlying issues.

FVD wants art and culture to become meaningful and connecting again, with attention to quality, craftsmanship, and our own local and historical culture.

FVD opposes structurally increasing expenditures or imposing expensive sustainability measures that lead to higher costs without demonstrable benefits for students, schools, or municipal finances.

167177.explanation: The municipality of Hengelo must cut unnecessary expenses, slim down its own organization, so residents have more money left in their wallets.

167161.explanation: Volt has a pragmatically motivated preference for a low-emission zone (LEZ/ULEZ) over a zero-emission zone (ZEZ). The camera system currently enforcing disruptive car traffic in the city center can be excellently applied for this purpose.

167162.explanation: There is space for two wind turbines in the A1/A35 junction, but only one wind turbine has had a plan submitted. Volt wants to work to get the second wind turbine here as well. To increase support for this turbine, Volt proposes it be owned by the residents of Hengelo.

167163.explanation: Hengelo spends about €2,000,000 less on Culture & Arts than expected based on the national average and population size. Volt believes this should be at least at the national average level. When Culture & Arts suffer, democracy suffers.

167164.explanation: As owner of these buildings, the municipality is responsible for their sustainability and accessibility. This is an investment in the future and quality of stay for our children.

167165.explanation: Equal opportunities in upbringing are very important. Where someone is born should not determine their chances. Equal opportunities begin with every child having the possibility to take music lessons. Music provides relaxation and brings people together.

167167.explanation: Yes, provided there is a need. Quality and maintenance are very important.

167168.explanation: Volt believes there should be more focus on building homes for the elderly. This also includes the option of so-called 'Knarrenhofjes' (senior compounds). Housing for the elderly promotes flow throughout the entire housing supply.

167169.explanation: Not everyone needs to depend on the car. In fact, cycling is healthy, and Hengelo's unique location as a regional rail hub makes healthier and more sustainable mobility possible even over longer distances.

167170.explanation: Volt considers this premise unrealistic. 30%-35% of total housing is already social rental. Wanting an additional 50% of all new builds to be affordable housing will result in a qualitatively poor housing stock in new neighborhoods.

167171.explanation: As a party, Volt has a broader vision document and policy position on the European housing market issues, including advocacy for affordable housing, repurposing vacant buildings, public housing ownership, regulating short-term rentals, and fiscal measures.

167172.explanation: Volt believes Hengelo should participate in Kansfonds projects. This prevents problems like in the childcare benefits scandal. Successful examples already exist in Zaanstad, Tilburg, and Amsterdam. This helps people find work faster and contribute more to their own development.

167173.explanation: Volt believes the municipality should invest in prevention, which is more efficient. Good education at schools and on social media can help. There is much shame around poverty, and asking for help is difficult. Approaching youth can lower the barrier to seeking help.

167174.explanation: Volt wants 30 km/h speed limits on all roads in built-up areas, except the main arteries. In principle, the city is for living, recreation, and meeting, not only the center. We see the car as a last resort for long distances or poorly accessible places by public transport.

167175.explanation: For most bus trips within Hengelo, Volt Hengelo considers cycling a better alternative. There are great initiatives in Hengelo to help low-income people get a bike and/or cycling lessons. Making the bus free is very expensive, and that money is better spent on promoting cycling and infrastructure.

167176.explanation: Volt believes a pleasant and safe cycling environment encourages more people to choose the bike over the car, which has health, environmental, and liveability benefits. Research in the Netherlands shows that safe, well-maintained cycling facilities can save lives.

167177.explanation: For most bus trips within Hengelo, Volt Hengelo considers cycling a better alternative. There are great initiatives in Hengelo to help low-income people get a bike and/or cycling lessons. Making the bus free is very expensive, and that money is better spent on promoting cycling and infrastructure.

167178.explanation: City centers where a larger share of people travel by car perform worse in sustainability and liveability, and especially economically. Therefore, Volt believes facilitating more car use is not a good plan.

167179.explanation: Volt Hengelo fears that extending Laan van Driene will cause it to serve as a shortcut from the A1 to Enschede and Enschede Kennispark, bringing even more traffic into the neighborhood instead of improving accessibility.

167180.explanation: Volt believes: Invest in people, not buildings. We make this possible in the environmental plan for new Wijkhuyzen; it is up to the residents to choose whether to implement it. Volt supports Wijkhuyzen and believes the municipality should ensure these are possible in zoning plans.

167181.explanation: Volt supports the approach chosen by Helsinki. The core of Helsinki’s homelessness policy is the Housing First principle: homeless people are given their own permanent home first, without having to be 'ready' with, for example, addiction care or other social problems beforehand.

167182.explanation: Volt thinks an Ombudsman is a good idea, but at the Twente regional level. This allows independent work. Embedding it within the own organization leads to the perception of the butcher judging his own meat.

167126.explanation: A think tank is good but not based on lottery selection.

167137.explanation: First seek free space in the cores.

167146.explanation: We need to move to 1.5 hours of free parking based on a parking disc.

167159.explanation: The owner must comply with the law and solve this problem themselves.

167181.explanation: Projects such as the birds of paradise homes must be initiated.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the construction of a new residential neighborhood outside the built-up area.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de bouw van een nieuwe wijk buiten de

titleParts.glossary: An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.pro: Some parties find this necessary because the housing shortage is severe. There are too few places in the city to build new homes. This way Hengelo can build new homes faster.
statements.con: Some parties believe there are still enough vacant sites in the city to build new homes. New construction outside the city damages the countryside and the nature that makes Hengelo so special. Hengelo should not grow to 100,000 inhabitants.

statements.title: On streets where cycle lanes are not unobstructed, the maximum speed should be reduced to 30 km/h.

titleParts.text: In straten waar fietspaden niet vrij liggen, moet de

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.

statements.pro: Some parties think this prevents dangerous situations, which promotes traffic safety. It makes the street clearer and more orderly.
statements.con: Some parties believe it is not always necessary for traffic safety to lower the speed limit to 30 km/h. It increases travel time for city buses on some routes.

statements.title: Hengelo should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.pro: A number of parties agree with this because they think it provides more safety. Residents will only behave properly—for example regarding traffic violations and illegal dumping—if rules are enforced. Municipal enforcement officers ensure people trust that rules are followed.
statements.con: A number of parties disagree with this because they believe the municipality can better ensure prevention. It should hire more youth workers. There are currently enough municipal enforcement officers (boa’s). It is unclear why more boa’s are needed.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.pro: Some parties believe this addresses the housing shortage. Converting offices into homes is too expensive without subsidies.
statements.con: Some parties believe this is not a task for the municipality. It is up to entrepreneurs and project developers to do this. The municipality can, as an incentive, make conversions easier or make the permit free.

statements.title: The Laan van Driene should be extended to end at Oldenzaalsestraat.
statements.moreInfo: The municipal council of Hengelo adopted a mobility plan in 2023. It calls the Verlengde Laan van Driene a “missing link” to direct city traffic to the A1. The Oldenzaalsestraat is an important road to the center that is currently heavily loaded, causing frequent congestion. Building the Verlengde Laan van Driene should relieve traffic on the Oldenzaalsestraat. It was decided to first investigate whether building the Verlengde Laan van Driene is really necessary.
statements.pro: A number of parties state that this leads to better traffic flow. It reduces through-traffic in surrounding neighborhoods. It improves accessibility.
statements.con: A number of parties argue that this leads to more traffic nuisance in Groot Driene. It is unnecessary, because traffic usually flows fairly well. It is unclear what consequences this measure has for the livability of the neighborhoods.

statements.title: Hengelo should, as soon as possible, allow only zero-emission cars in the city center.
statements.pro: Some parties think this is good for livability in the city center. It is good for the environment.
statements.con: Some parties believe that already almost no cars are allowed into the city center. This makes the city center less accessible, which can have consequences for the local economy.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.pro: Some parties agree because they believe socially useful work helps people get paid employment. There should be reciprocity: if you receive something, you should give something back.
statements.con: Some parties disagree because they believe that work as a counter-performance is not the purpose of a social assistance benefit. That benefit guarantees a minimum to survive, whatever your circumstances. Those who work should receive wages for it, not a benefit.

statements.title: The municipality should buy out and demolish the Twente asphalt plant.
statements.moreInfo: There is an asphalt plant in Hengelo. It is technically difficult for the plant to always meet all environmental requirements, and sometimes those limits are exceeded. To protect the health and safety of the surroundings, the municipality is legally obliged to take action when standards for very hazardous substances are violated.
statements.pro: Some parties support this because it would end the smell and air pollution that is harmful to residents' health. The asphalt plant has often already received heavy fines for violating environmental rules. That proves the company cannot operate in Hengelo.
statements.con: Some parties oppose this because they believe the municipality should enforce the rules. And if the company still does not comply, the municipality should close it. Buying out a company rewards bad behavior.

statements.title: At least 35% of newly built homes in Hengelo should be social rental housing. Currently, 30% of homes in Hengelo are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Hengelo moet minstens 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Hengelo is nu 30% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.pro: A number of parties agree because there is a great need for social rental housing. This is visible from the long waiting lists. In recent years the number of social rental homes has declined because homes have been demolished or sold.
statements.con: A number of parties disagree because they think the current target is sufficient. The municipality now wants 30% of new construction to be social rental housing. A rate of 35% leads to more rules and therefore to less being built.

statements.title: The municipality should ensure that not one but two wind turbines are built.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet ervoor zorgen dat er niet één, maar twee

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.pro: Some parties agree because they believe wind turbines contribute to the switch to sustainable energy. Wind turbines take up less space than solar fields. On the site where one wind turbine is planned now, there is room for a second one.
statements.con: Some parties disagree because they believe too few residents support wind turbines. There is too little space in Hengelo to place another wind turbine. Decisions about placing wind turbines are made by the province, not the municipality.

statements.title: A group of residents selected by lottery should advise the municipality on important issues.
statements.moreInfo: The group referred to is usually called a citizens' assembly. The municipality can organize such an assembly. In 2023 the Hengelo municipal council adopted a participation ordinance setting the rules for citizen participation and thus for a citizens' assembly. The municipality must make clear in advance exactly what the assembly will advise on and how the council will take the assembly’s outcome into account in its final decision.
statements.pro: A number of parties are in favor because such a group of citizens (a citizens' assembly) increases support for municipal council decisions. It gives residents influence over decisions that affect them. It produces better decisions.
statements.con: A number of parties are against this because not all residents who want to participate would be allowed to. Only citizens who are drawn by lot would be allowed to speak. Advice should come from experts and not from a random group of residents. The municipal council is elected to represent residents. A citizens’ assembly is therefore not necessary.

statements.title: Fines for dumping waste should be increased.
statements.pro: Some parties think polluters should be dealt with strictly. This is a good measure against decline and thus improves the city's livability. A lot of waste is currently dumped in the city.
statements.con: Some parties believe fines do not help to combat illegal dumping of waste. The fines are already high. Increasing the chance of being caught is more important than increasing the fines.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance. More people would then receive help.
statements.pro: A number of parties support this because then working residents who are just above the income threshold can also receive help. More and more residents need poverty assistance.
statements.con: A number of parties are against this because Hengelo already has a high income threshold for poverty assistance. Tailored solutions work better than raising the income threshold.

statements.title: Every neighborhood in Hengelo should get its own neighborhood community center.
statements.moreInfo: Hengelo has three community houses (Wijkhuyzen) where residents can go for activities, help, and information. These Wijkhuyzen are in Hasselo, Nijverheid, and Weidedorp.
statements.pro: A number of parties think this is a good idea because Wijkhuyzen foster meetings between residents and social cohesion. In Wijkhuyzen the municipality can organize care close to home.
statements.con: A number of parties think this is not a good idea because not every neighborhood has the same needs. Residents should indicate themselves whether they want a neighborhood house.

statements.title: There should be more free short-term parking spaces in the city center.
statements.pro: Some parties support this because it makes the city center more attractive to visitors. It increases the city's economic viability. It supports local entrepreneurs.
statements.con: Some parties oppose this because there are currently enough free short-term parking spaces. Encouraging cycling and public transport to visit the city center is more important. City centers where a larger share of people go by car perform worse economically.

statements.title: In Hengelo, homeless people should be given priority when allocating social rental housing.

titleParts.text: In Hengelo moeten daklozen voorrang krijgen bij de toewijzing van

titleParts.glossary: Rental homes with low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.pro: Parties that agree with this believe that housing is the most important thing for homeless people. Only by providing a roof over their heads can the municipality give homeless people a stable and safe base. It does not suit Hengelo that people are living on the streets.
statements.con: Parties that disagree believe that giving priority to homeless people is not fair to other housing seekers. A home is not always the best solution to a homeless person's problems.

statements.title: The municipality should fund free buses between the neighborhoods and the city center on Saturdays.
statements.pro: Some parties agree with this because it attracts extra visitors to the city center. That is good for the local economy. It helps reduce loneliness. It reduces parking pressure in the city center.
statements.con: Some parties disagree because they believe cycling to the city center should be preferred. It is an expensive measure that must be paid for by residents. It is better to arrange this only for residents with low incomes.

statements.title: The municipality of Hengelo should stop collecting residual waste and packaging waste (PMD) separately.

titleParts.text: De gemeente Hengelo moet stoppen met het apart inzamelen van

titleParts.glossary: Waste that remains after paper, plastic and organic waste have been collected separately.

statements.moreInfo: In September 2024 the collection of plastic packaging, metal packaging and drink cartons (PMD) changed for high-rise buildings. The above-ground PMD containers were removed and replaced by “drop posts” for bag collection. This collection method has caused a lot of litter and nuisance. In January the council decided to change the system from 2027. The exact form of the change is yet to be decided by the council.
statements.pro: Parties that agree say that sorting waste afterwards leads to better results. The current way of disposing of waste is too complicated for residents. The current method leads to waste dumping and piles of debris in the neighborhood.
statements.con: Parties that disagree say it is more sustainable if residents separate their waste in advance. Having residents separate their waste beforehand, as happens now, is cheaper. For low-rise housing, residents separating their waste in advance works fine.

statements.title: The municipality should take part in the annual Pride week for all LGBTQIA+ residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meedoen met de jaarlijkse Prideweek voor alle

titleParts.glossary: The letters stand for lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, intersex and asexual. The + stands for all other ways people experience their identity and sexuality.

statements.pro: A number of parties agree because this contributes to an inclusive society. The municipality thus shows that all residents are treated equally and everyone can feel at home and safe. LGBTQIA+ people are still regularly discriminated against.
statements.con: A number of parties disagree because LGBTQIA+ people should not receive a separate platform. The municipality can also demonstrate inclusion and connection in other ways.

statements.title: The municipality should spend less money on integrating refugees who have a residence permit.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet minder geld uitgeven aan de

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.
Refugees who have been granted a residence permit and are therefore allowed to stay in the Netherlands.

statements.pro: Some parties agree because they believe newcomers themselves are responsible for their integration. The municipality must scrutinize how the money is spent.
statements.con: Some parties disagree because they believe these newcomers integrate faster with municipal support. They also find work more quickly. Supporting status holders in their integration is a legal duty and a moral obligation.

statements.title: When the municipality purchases goods or services, it should give priority to sustainable companies.
statements.pro: Some parties support this because the municipality has already promised to do so. It is called socially responsible procurement. Sustainable procurement is part of that. The municipality must set a good example.
statements.con: Some parties oppose this because value for money must come first when the municipality buys goods or services. The municipality should purchase as much as possible from Hengelo businesses.

statements.title: Every household should pay a fixed fee for waste collection; the amount of waste you put out would not matter.
statements.pro: A number of parties agree because they think this will reduce dumping of waste. Currently, residents who dispose of more waste have to pay more. A fixed fee is fair. Everyone then pays the same for the same basic service.
statements.con: A number of parties disagree because they find this unfair. It is not fair if people who deliver little waste have to pay the same as those who deliver a lot. If it is more expensive to deliver more waste, you encourage residents to produce less waste.

statements.title: There should be more places to skateboard in Hengelo.
statements.pro: Some parties agree because it invites young people to be active outdoors and meet others. The skateboard park behind the station is a great success. Groot Driene could also use a place like that.
statements.con: Some parties disagree because there is already a nice skateboard park behind the station. Building and maintaining a new skate facility costs a lot of money.

statements.title: The municipality should mow public grass more often.
statements.pro: A number of parties agree because public spaces should be tidy. The public space then becomes more accessible. Mowing more often is better for traffic safety.
statements.con: A number of parties disagree because they think it is better to give herbs and flowers a chance to grow. Not mowing the grass or mowing it later is good for insects and birds. Not mowing the grass or mowing it later is good for the liveability of Hengelo.

statements.title: Half of the newly built homes should be owner-occupied properties costing no more than €250,000.
statements.pro: A number of parties agree because this helps first-time buyers to obtain an owner-occupied home. It is the municipality's task to ensure affordable housing. This will facilitate housing turnover.
statements.con: A number of parties disagree because this leads to homebuyers having little choice. Mainly houses costing less than €250,000 would be built. It is almost impossible to build a house for €250,000. It does not help the flow on the housing market.

statements.title: The municipality should invest more money in making existing school buildings more sustainable.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld steken in het

titleParts.glossary: A building with low energy use or built with materials that can be (re)used for a long time.

statements.pro: Parties that agree point out that this ensures a healthy and safe indoor climate in school buildings. In addition, the energy bill will be lower.
statements.con: Parties that disagree point out that there is already a plan for the sustainability and new construction of school buildings. Spending more money than provided for in that plan is unnecessary.

statements.title: The municipality should accommodate more asylum seekers than is legally required.

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

statements.moreInfo: Under the Distribution Act Hengelo must accommodate 415 asylum seekers; Ukrainians are not counted in this. According to the municipality, in 2024 there were 470 asylum seekers in Hengelo’s reception, spread over three locations: Havezatelaan, Hazenweg and Van der Valk.
statements.pro: A number of parties agree because Hengelo already does this, and it is going well. Hengelo is welcoming to refugees.
statements.con: A number of parties disagree because Hengelo does not have to take on the responsibility of other municipalities. Other municipalities must also take in asylum seekers now. Some parties believe Hengelo should not take in asylum seekers at all.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.pro: Some parties agree because they believe priority for local residents is fair. People are best off living where they work or where their family and friends are.
statements.con: Some parties disagree because they believe it is good for Hengelo's development if people from outside Hengelo move here. It is unfair: everyone should have an equal chance to get a social rental home.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the construction of homes without parking spaces.
statements.pro: Some parties agree because there is not always room for a parking space and not everyone needs one. We should use public space for trees, plants and lawns. If there are fewer rules, more can be built.
statements.con: Some parties disagree because this causes more cars to be parked in the surrounding neighborhoods. Each home should have at least one parking space. People from Hengelo also want to keep driving their own car in the future.

statements.title: There should be a ban on fatbikes in the city center.
statements.pro: Parties that agree believe fatbikes cause unsafe situations and accidents. A ban would put an end to this. Other municipalities are working on a fatbike ban.
statements.con: Parties that disagree say it's not about the fatbike itself, but about the rider of the fatbike. It is not possible to ban fatbikes.

shootoutStatements.title: When maintaining streets, the municipality must give priority to cycle paths.
The municipality must pay for public transport within Hengelo for residents with low incomes.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.
Hengelo must procure as much as possible from social enterprises.
Low-income residents must find it easier to request additional financial support from the municipality.
The municipality may only install a district heating network in neighborhoods where at least 70% of households want to switch.
The municipality must pay for music lessons for children from poor families.
Hengelo must appoint an ombudsman for complaints about the municipality.
There must be CCTV surveillance in bicycle tunnels.
The municipality must do more to detect and help young people with debts.
The municipality must lobby the national government to locate the new Twente hospital in Hengelo.
It must remain possible to pay in cash at the municipal service desk.
The municipality must make the first hour of parking in the city centre free.
The municipality must cut spending on hiring external personnel.
The municipality must financially support housing corporation Welbions in building homes with rents of €900 to €1,200.
The municipality must stop spending money on projects to create more urban nature.
The municipality must make land available to groups of older people who want to build homes together.
The municipality must spend less on arts and culture.
